Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. A Decade of Slug 675 upvotes, 65 comments
  2. More than 135 open hardware devices flashable with your own firmware 290 upvotes, 36 comments
  3. A tale about fixing eBPF spinlock issues in the Linux kernel 126 upvotes, 13 comments
  4. Nightingale – open-source karaoke app that works with any song on your computer 241 upvotes, 41 comments
  5. Write up of my homebrew CPU build 138 upvotes, 21 comments
  6. Unsloth Studio 335 upvotes, 64 comments
  7. (Media over QUIC) on a Boat 65 upvotes, 9 comments
  8. A Tiny Camera Revealed a Hidden Passage in the Great Pyramid 14 upvotes, 0 comments
  9. Using calculus to do number theory 21 upvotes, 1 comments
  10. Mistral AI Releases Forge 602 upvotes, 144 comments
  11. JPEG Compression 313 upvotes, 77 comments
  12. Show HN: Sub-millisecond VM sandboxes using CoW memory forking 222 upvotes, 56 comments
  13. Microsoft's 'unhackable' Xbox One has been hacked by 'Bliss' 738 upvotes, 275 comments
  14. Celebrating Tony Hoare's mark on computer science 71 upvotes, 19 comments
  15. The pleasures of poor product design 179 upvotes, 61 comments
  16. It Took Me 30 Years to Solve This VFX Problem – Green Screen Problem [video] 270 upvotes, 104 comments
  17. Pandas Exercises for Data Analysis (Interactive) 54 upvotes, 15 comments
  18. Electron microscopy shows ‘mouse bite’ defects in semiconductors 92 upvotes, 30 comments
  19. How the Eon Team Produced a Virtual Embodied Fly 50 upvotes, 14 comments
  20. Tech hobbyist makes shoulder-mounted guided missile prototype with $96 in parts 54 upvotes, 16 comments
  21. Show HN: Pgit – A Git-like CLI backed by PostgreSQL 82 upvotes, 30 comments
  22. Get Shit Done: A meta-prompting, context engineering and spec-driven dev system 378 upvotes, 197 comments
  23. Ryugu asteroid samples contain all DNA and RNA building blocks 279 upvotes, 147 comments
  24. Rob Pike's 5 Rules of Programming 364 upvotes, 197 comments
  25. Have a fucking website 628 upvotes, 357 comments
  26. Python 3.15's JIT is now back on track 427 upvotes, 249 comments
  27. Aliens.gov ~ domain registered 17MAR2026 49 upvotes, 33 comments
  28. Federal Cyber Experts Called Microsoft's Cloud "A Pile of Shit", yet Approved It 5 upvotes, 2 comments
  29. Pardoned for Fraud, a CEO Mounts His Comeback: 'We Can Trust You Now' 32 upvotes, 37 comments
  30. Ndea (YC W26) is hiring a symbolic RL search guidance lead 0 upvotes, 0 comments

Last generated 10:30pm, 18 March 2026. Source here.
